note: Modeler API is a framework module: it defines Modeler + ModelOwner plugin types, three YAML plugin types, and a central Api mediator service. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site using Modeler API, what are the two core plugin types it defines and what does each side represent? Which PHP attributes declare them?
evals.expected_output: A Modeler plugin (#[Modeler], namespace Plugin/ModelerApiModeler) provides the visual editor; a ModelOwner plugin (#[ModelOwner], namespace Plugin/ModelerApiModelOwner) owns the config entities being modeled. The Api service mediates between them.

evals.prompt: In the Modeler API ecosystem, is the bpmn_io module a Modeler or a Model Owner, and how does it register itself?
evals.expected_output: bpmn_io is a Modeler: it registers a plugin with the #[Modeler] attribute in Plugin/ModelerApiModeler implementing ModelerInterface (extending ModelerBase). It does not define a plugin type.

evals.prompt: Which Modeler API plugin type do ECA (via eca_ui) and AI Agents implement so their configurations can be graphically edited?
evals.expected_output: They implement the ModelOwner plugin type (ModelOwnerInterface, #[ModelOwner]); they are model owners whose config entities any modeler can edit.

evals.prompt: In code on a Drupal 11 site, which Modeler API service mediates between modelers and model owners, and which method finds the model owner plugin for a given config entity?
evals.expected_output: The modeler_api.service service (Drupal\modeler_api\Api). Api::findOwner($model) returns the ModelOwnerInterface plugin that owns a config entity.

evals.prompt: Modeler API translates between owners and modelers using a fixed set of generic component types. Name them and where the constants live.
evals.expected_output: Seven types as Api::COMPONENT_TYPE_* constants (ints 1-7): start, subprocess, swimlane, element, link, gateway, annotation. They live on Drupal\modeler_api\Api (COMPONENT_TYPE_NAMES maps them to YAML keys).

evals.prompt: Where is Modeler API configured and what are the three ways raw modeler diagram data can be stored?
evals.expected_output: Configured at /admin/config/workflow/modeler_api (route modeler_api.settings). Raw data storage per owner/modeler: as third-party settings on the config entity, in a separate config entity, or not stored at all.

evals.prompt: Besides Modeler and ModelOwner, which three YAML-defined plugin types does Modeler API provide, and what YAML file names discover them?
evals.expected_output: Context (MODULE.modeler_api.contexts.yml), Dependency (MODULE.modeler_api.dependencies.yml), and Template Token (MODULE.modeler_api.template_tokens.yml) — all YAML, no PHP.

evals.prompt: How do I export a Modeler API model as a Drupal recipe from the command line, and which owner/model does it take?
evals.expected_output: drush modeler_api:model:export <owner_id> <id> exports the model as a recipe; --namespace and --destination options customize it. The Api/ExportRecipe service backs it.
